parameters.name: group
parameters.label: Group
parameters.description: If there are input groups, specifying a group name in this field will cause this to act only upon the group specified. Accepts patterns, as described in .

parameters.name: unit
parameters.label: Make Normals Unit Length
parameters.description: Checking this option will normalize the length of normals to a length of one unit.

parameters.name: prenml
parameters.label: Compute Normals
parameters.description: Checking this option means that the surface normals will be computed. Where points are shared between polygons, smooth shading results, and where points are not shared (unique), faceted edges result.

parameters.name: unique
parameters.label: Unique Points
parameters.description: Makes each vertex have a unique point. The result of selecting this option is that all vertices are made into unique points, thus making all edges hard, with no smooth shading.

parameters.name: cons
parameters.label: Consolidate
parameters.description: Consolidate eliminates the redundancy of having many points that are close to each other, by merging them together to form a fewer set of common points. Consolidate is useful for cleaning up an edge t

parameters.name: dist
parameters.label: Distance
parameters.description: Points and normals less than this distance apart will be consolidated, or have their normals averaged, based on the setting in the Consolidate menu.

parameters.name: inline
parameters.label: Remove Inline Points
parameters.description: Removes points that lie inline with its neighboring points.

parameters.name: inlinedist
parameters.label: Distance
parameters.description: Set the distance threshold for removing inline points when the above parameter is On.

parameters.name: orientpolys
parameters.label: Orient Polygons
parameters.description: Orients all polygons so they have the same winding direction.

parameters.name: cusp
parameters.label: Cusp Polygons
parameters.description: Most of the time, you want some polygons to be smooth shaded and others to be faceted. Usually polygons that meet at low angles should be smooth shaded, and polygon edges that meet at sharper angles s

parameters.name: angle
parameters.label: Cusp Angle
parameters.description: Cusping allows you to specify the threshold angle at which the edges become faceted. A good typical value is 20.

parameters.name: remove
parameters.label: Remove Degenerate
parameters.description: Sometimes (not often) your geometry can get messed up, where there are points hanging around that are not used for anything, or there are primitives that don't make sense. This option checks for these

parameters.name: postnml
parameters.label: Compute Normals
parameters.description: Again, allows you to compute the normals after the consolidation or cusping stages. You should select this if you have set either the Cusp or Consolidate option.
